title: Pre-Columbian Native Societies
description: Diverse Native American societies with complex political, economic, and social structures across North America

title: Maize Cultivation Spreads
description: Agricultural development supports population growth and settled societies

title: Rise of Mississippian Culture
description: Large urban centers like Cahokia emerge with trade networks and mound-building

title: Christopher Columbus Arrives in Americas
description: Beginning of sustained European contact with the Americas

title: Columbian Exchange Begins
description: Transfer of plants, animals, diseases, and people between the Old and New Worlds

title: Treaty of Tordesillas
description: Spain and Portugal divide newly discovered lands outside Europe

title: Spanish Conquest of Aztec Empire
description: Hernán Cortés defeats the Aztecs with military force, alliances, and disease

title: Spanish Conquest of Inca Empire
description: Francisco Pizarro conquers the Inca, expanding Spanish control in South America

title: Encomienda System Established
description: Spanish labor system exploiting Native Americans for labor and tribute

title: Introduction of African Slavery to Americas
description: Africans are forcibly brought to the Americas as part of the Atlantic slave trade

title: St. Augustine Founded
description: First permanent European settlement in what becomes the United States

title: Roanoke Colony Founded
description: English attempt at colonization that mysteriously disappears

title: Joint-Stock Companies Form
description: Investors pool funds and resources to fund colonization efforts

title: Jamestown Founded
description: First permanent English settlement in North America

title: Development of Mercantilism
description: Economic system where colonies exist to benefit the mother country

title: Spread of Christianity in Americas
description: Missionary efforts convert Native Americans, often by force

title: Leif Erikson Reaches Vinland
description: Norse explorer Leif Erikson establishes short-lived settlement in North America, predating Columbus

title: Iroquois Confederacy Forms
description: Haudenosaunee alliance of Native nations creates a powerful political union based on the Great Law of Peace

title: Virgin Soil Epidemics
description: European diseases such as smallpox cause massive Native American population decline due to lack of immunity

title: New Laws of 1542 Enacted
description: Spanish crown attempts to limit abuse of Native Americans and reform the encomienda system

title: Headright System Introduced
description: Virginia policy granting land to settlers who financed passage, expanding indentured servitude and colonial growth

title: House of Burgesses Established
description: First representative legislative assembly in colonial America, forming early self-government in Virginia

title: Arrival of First Enslaved Africans in Virginia
description: Marks the beginning of institutionalized African slavery in English North America

title: Mayflower Compact Signed
description: Early self-government agreement among Pilgrims in Plymouth Colony

title: Plymouth Colony Founded
description: Religious refuge colony established in New England

title: Great Puritan Migration
description: Mass migration of Puritans to New England seeking religious autonomy

title: Pequot War
description: Early conflict between English settlers and Pequot tribe leading to Pequot destruction

title: King Philip's War
description: Major conflict between Native Americans and New England colonists over land control

title: Bacon's Rebellion
description: Uprising in Virginia highlighting tensions between frontier settlers and colonial elites

title: Dominion of New England Established
description: Royal consolidation of New England colonies under tighter English control

title: Glorious Revolution Impacts Colonies
description: Political upheaval in England increases colonial resistance to royal authority

title: Salem Witch Trials
description: Mass hysteria in Massachusetts resulting in executions of accused witches

title: Zenger Trial
description: Case that strengthened freedom of the press in colonial America

title: First Great Awakening
description: Religious revival emphasizing emotional worship and challenging established churches

title: Stono Rebellion
description: Largest slave uprising in colonial America, occurring in South Carolina

title: Albany Plan of Union Proposed
description: Early attempt by Benjamin Franklin to unify colonies for defense

title: French and Indian War Begins
description: Conflict between Britain and France over control of North America

title: French and Indian War Begins
description: Conflict between Britain and France over North American territory

title: Albany Plan of Union
description: Early attempt to unify colonies for defense and coordination

title: Treaty of Paris
description: Ends French and Indian War; Britain gains most French territory in North America

title: Proclamation of 1763
description: Restricts colonial settlement west of Appalachian Mountains

title: Stamp Act
description: Direct tax that triggers widespread colonial resistance

title: Townshend Acts
description: Taxes on imports leading to colonial boycotts

title: Boston Massacre
description: Violent clash used as propaganda against Britain

title: Boston Tea Party
description: Colonial protest against Tea Act by destroying British tea

title: Intolerable Acts
description: Punitive laws that unify colonial resistance

title: First Continental Congress
description: Colonies coordinate resistance to British policies

title: Lexington and Concord
description: First battles of the American Revolution

title: Declaration of Independence
description: Colonies formally declare independence from Britain

title: Battle of Saratoga
description: Turning point of the Revolution; secures French alliance

title: Articles of Confederation Ratified
description: First U.S. government framework; weak central authority

title: Treaty of Paris (Revolutionary War Ends)
description: Britain recognizes U.S. independence

title: Shays' Rebellion
description: Revolt highlighting weaknesses of the Articles of Confederation

title: Constitutional Convention
description: Delegates draft new U.S. Constitution

title: U.S. Constitution Ratified
description: Establishes stronger federal government

title: Bill of Rights Adopted
description: Guarantees individual liberties and limits government power

title: Whiskey Rebellion
description: Tax protest tested federal authority under Washington

title: XYZ Affair
description: Diplomatic crisis with France leading to Quasi-War

title: Alien and Sedition Acts
description: Restrict immigration and free speech; sparks major political conflict

title: Virginia and Kentucky Resolutions
description: Argue states can nullify unconstitutional federal laws

title: Election of 1800
description: Peaceful transfer of power from Federalists to Democratic-Republicans, establishing democratic stability

title: Louisiana Purchase
description: U.S. doubles its territory by purchasing land from France under Jefferson

title: Marbury v. Madison
description: Establishes judicial review, strengthening the Supreme Court's power

title: Lewis and Clark Expedition
description: Exploration of the Louisiana Territory to map and study the West

title: Embargo Act of 1807
description: Jefferson's attempt to avoid war by stopping trade with foreign nations, harming the U.S. economy

title: War of 1812 Begins
description: Conflict between the U.S. and Britain over trade restrictions and impressment

title: Hartford Convention
description: Federalist meeting expressing opposition to War of 1812, damaging the party's reputation

title: Treaty of Ghent
description: Ends the War of 1812 and restores pre-war boundaries

title: Era of Good Feelings Begins
description: Period of political unity under Monroe following the collapse of the Federalist Party

title: Missouri Compromise
description: Balances slave and free states and sets 36°30′ line for slavery expansion

title: Monroe Doctrine
description: U.S. declares opposition to European colonization in the Americas

title: Market Revolution Begins
description: Economic transformation driven by industrialization, transportation improvements, and commercial farming

title: Erie Canal Completed
description: Connects Great Lakes to Atlantic Ocean, boosting trade and western expansion

title: Second Great Awakening
description: Religious revival promoting individual salvation and social reform movements

title: Andrew Jackson Elected President
description: Marks rise of Jacksonian democracy and expanded suffrage for white men

title: Indian Removal Act
description: Authorizes forced relocation of Native American tribes west of the Mississippi River

title: Trail of Tears
description: Forced relocation of Cherokee Nation resulting in thousands of deaths

title: Nullification Crisis
description: Conflict between South Carolina and federal government over tariff laws

title: Bank War
description: Andrew Jackson dismantles the Second Bank of the United States

title: Panic of 1837
description: Severe economic depression triggered by speculation and banking instability

title: Seneca Falls Convention
description: First women's rights convention in the U.S., launching organized feminist movement

title: Mexican-American War Begins
description: War between U.S. and Mexico over territorial expansion

title: Manifest Destiny Popularized
description: Belief that U.S. expansion across North America is justified and inevitable
